molecular_profile: Sporadic (non-hereditary, rule out MEN2 pending germline testing)

treatments.name: Selpercatinib (Retevmo) — Selective RET Inhibitor

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT LIBRETTO-531 (NEJM 2023, n=291) demonstrated clear superiority over cabozantinib/vandetanib. Phase 1/2 LIBRETTO-001 (n=531) provided long-term durability data at 42+ months follow-up. FDA traditional approval September 2024

survival_benefit.rationale: LIBRETTO-531: median PFS not reached vs 16.8 months for MKIs (HR 0.28, p<0.001). ORR 69.4% vs 38.8%. LIBRETTO-001: 82.5% ORR in treatment-naive MTC, median PFS not reached at 42.4 months. 18-month OS rate 95.5% vs 92.8%. Dramatic improvement over prior standard MKIs

accessibility.rationale: FDA traditional approval September 2024 for adults and pediatric patients >=2 years with advanced/metastatic RET-mutant MTC. Widely available at oncology centers. Covered by major insurance plans

safety_profile.rationale: Favorable safety vs MKIs. Grade >=3 AEs: 52.8% vs 76.3% control. Dose reduction 38.9% vs 77.3%. Discontinuation 4.7% vs 26.8%. Main toxicities: hypertension (21% grade >=3), diarrhea, dry mouth, ALT/AST elevation. Better tolerated than cabozantinib/vandetanib

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient has RET M918T somatic mutation — the most common RET mutation in MTC (62.7% of LIBRETTO-531 patients). Selpercatinib directly targets the RET kinase. Perfect biomarker match

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Highly selective, ATP-competitive small-molecule inhibitor of RET receptor tyrosine kinase. Potently inhibits wild-type RET and multiple mutant forms including M918T, V804L/M gatekeeper mutations, and CCDC6-RET fusions. Blocks constitutive RET signaling driving tumor proliferation and survival through MAPK, PI3K/AKT, and JAK/STAT pathways.
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (Traditional approval September 2024) — new first-line standard for RET-mutant MTC

treatments.name: Total Thyroidectomy + Central/Lateral Neck Dissection (Primary Surgery)

evidence_level.rationale: Gold-standard treatment per NCCN v1.2025, ATA 2015 guidelines, and ESMO guidelines. Decades of prospective and retrospective evidence supporting total thyroidectomy with compartment-oriented neck dissection as primary curative therapy for MTC

survival_benefit.rationale: For T3N1a MTC, biochemical cure (undetectable calcitonin) achievable in approximately 30-40% of cases. 10-year disease-specific survival 86% with adequate surgery. 5-year survival 93% for stages I-III. Curative intent — the only treatment modality that can potentially cure MTC

accessibility.rationale: Universally available at any endocrine/head-and-neck surgical center worldwide. No drug costs or biomarker requirements for eligibility. Standard first-line intervention

safety_profile.rationale: Risks include recurrent laryngeal nerve injury (2-5%), permanent hypoparathyroidism (1-5%), postoperative hypocalcemia. Requires lifelong thyroid hormone replacement. Central neck dissection increases complication rates modestly vs thyroidectomy alone

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal — applicable to all MTC patients regardless of RET status. Surgery is required for all operable MTC

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Complete surgical extirpation of the thyroid gland with en-bloc removal of central compartment (level VI) lymph nodes, and ipsilateral or bilateral lateral neck dissection (levels II-V) as indicated by preoperative imaging and intraoperative findings. Aims to achieve R0 resection and biochemical cure (undetectable calcitonin).

key_evidence.study_name: ATA Guidelines Task Force on MTC / NCCN Thyroid Carcinoma v1.2025

treatments.availability: Universally available — standard of care at all surgical centers

treatments.name: Cabozantinib (Cometriq) — Multi-Kinase Inhibitor

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT EXAM trial (n=330) published in JCO 2013. FDA approved November 2012 for progressive, metastatic MTC. Mature overall survival data available with specific M918T subgroup analysis

survival_benefit.rationale: EXAM: median PFS 11.2 vs 4.0 months (HR 0.28, p<0.001). ORR 28% vs 0%. OS 26.6 vs 21.1 months (HR 0.85, NS). Critically, in RET M918T subgroup: OS 44.3 vs 18.9 months (HR 0.60, p=0.03) — significant survival advantage in this patient's molecular subtype

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved since November 2012. Widely available globally. Well-established prescribing patterns and insurance coverage. No companion diagnostic required

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity burden: diarrhea (63%), palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia (50%), weight loss (48%), decreased appetite (46%), nausea (43%), fatigue (41%). Grade >=3 AEs common. Dose reduction needed in 79% of patients. Substantially worse tolerability than selpercatinib

biomarker_match.rationale: Approved for all MTC regardless of RET status, but shows enhanced efficacy in RET M918T subgroup (OS 44.3 vs 18.9 months). Multi-target activity hits RET, VEGFR2, MET, and other kinases relevant to MTC biology

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Oral multi-kinase inhibitor targeting RET, VEGFR1/2/3, MET, KIT, FLT3, AXL, and TIE2. Inhibits tumor angiogenesis via VEGFR blockade, directly targets RET-driven proliferation, and suppresses MET-mediated invasion and metastasis. Broader kinase spectrum provides anti-angiogenic effects but also drives more off-target toxicity.
biomarker_requirements: None required (approved for all progressive metastatic MTC)
Enhanced benefit in RET M918T positive tumors
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (November 2012) — established option for progressive metastatic MTC

treatments.name: Vandetanib (Caprelsa) — Multi-Kinase Inhibitor

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT ZETA trial (n=331) published in JCO 2012. First FDA-approved drug for MTC (April 2011). Post-hoc analysis in progressive/symptomatic subgroup published 2020. Long-term follow-up data available

survival_benefit.rationale: ZETA: median PFS 30.5 vs 19.3 months (HR 0.46, p<0.001). ORR 45% vs 13%. However, no overall survival benefit demonstrated (HR 0.89) likely due to crossover. Post-hoc analysis in symptomatic/progressive patients also showed no OS benefit (HR 1.08). Less evidence for M918T-specific benefit than cabozantinib

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved since April 2011, but availability has become restricted in some markets. REMS program required due to QTc prolongation risk. Currently limited to RET-mutated patients in some prescribing contexts

safety_profile.rationale: Diarrhea (56%), rash (45%), nausea (33%), hypertension (32%). Significant QTc prolongation risk requiring ECG monitoring and REMS certification. Grade >=3 diarrhea (11%), hypertension (9%). Risk of torsades de pointes. Less tolerable than selpercatinib

biomarker_match.rationale: Originally approved for all MTC but now increasingly restricted to RET-mutated patients. Targets RET, VEGFR, and EGFR. Active against M918T but without the specific M918T subgroup survival data that cabozantinib has demonstrated

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Oral multi-kinase inhibitor targeting RET, VEGFR2/3, and EGFR. Inhibits RET-driven tumor proliferation, blocks VEGF-mediated angiogenesis, and suppresses EGFR signaling. The EGFR inhibition contributes to dermatologic toxicities (rash, photosensitivity) but may also provide additional anti-tumor activity.
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (April 2011) — REMS program required. Second-line to selpercatinib per updated guidelines

treatments.name: Pralsetinib (Gavreto) — Selective RET Inhibitor

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2 ARROW trial (n=175 safety population) demonstrated robust efficacy. Originally received FDA accelerated approval December 2020 for RET-mutant MTC. However, US indication voluntarily withdrawn 2024 as phase 3 AcceleRET-MTC confirmatory trial was deemed infeasible. Remains available outside the US

survival_benefit.rationale: ARROW: ORR 71% in treatment-naive MTC, 60% in previously treated MTC. Median PFS 25.8 months in previously treated patients, not reached in treatment-naive. Median duration of response not reached in treatment-naive cohort. Comparable efficacy to selpercatinib Phase 1/2 data

accessibility.rationale: US indication withdrawn in 2024. No longer commercially available in the United States. Still approved and accessible in the EU, Japan, and other markets. US patients cannot access outside of remaining compassionate use

safety_profile.rationale: 97.1% reported TRAEs; discontinuation in 5.7%, dose reduction in 52.6%, one treatment-related death (0.6%). Grade >=3: hypertension (17%), neutropenia (13%), lymphopenia (12%), anemia (10%). Comparable safety to selpercatinib but higher neutropenia

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets RET M918T and other RET activating mutations. Selective RET inhibitor with high potency against the patient's exact somatic mutation. Ideal biomarker match

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Highly selective, ATP-competitive inhibitor of RET receptor tyrosine kinase. Designed for enhanced potency and selectivity against oncogenic RET alterations including point mutations (M918T, V804L/M) and gene fusions. Blocks RET autophosphorylation and downstream MAPK/ERK and PI3K/AKT signaling.
biomarker_requirements: RET activating mutation or RET fusion (confirmed: M918T present)
treatments.availability: US indication voluntarily withdrawn (2024). Available in EU, Japan, and other markets. Not accessible for new US patients

treatments.name: External Beam Radiation Therapy (EBRT) — Adjuvant/Palliative

evidence_level.rationale: Retrospective data and guideline recommendations (NCCN, ATA). No randomized trials. Used adjuvantly for high-risk features (T3/T4, extranodal extension, positive margins) or palliatively for symptomatic metastases. Evidence supports local control improvement

survival_benefit.rationale: Improves locoregional control but no demonstrated overall survival benefit in MTC. Primarily used to reduce local recurrence risk in patients with R1/R2 resection or T3/T4 disease. For this T3N1a patient, adjuvant EBRT may be considered if residual disease or close margins

accessibility.rationale: Universally available at radiation oncology centers worldwide. Standard equipment (IMRT/VMAT). No biomarker or drug requirements. Insurance universally covers adjuvant radiation

safety_profile.rationale: Acute: dysphagia, skin erythema, fatigue, mucositis. Late: fibrosis, esophageal stricture, xerostomia, hypothyroidism exacerbation. Risk of radiation-induced damage to surrounding structures in the neck. Modern IMRT/VMAT reduces toxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal — applicable regardless of molecular profile. Used based on anatomic/pathologic criteria (T stage, margin status, extranodal extension), not biomarkers

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Delivers targeted ionizing radiation to the thyroid bed and involved nodal basins using intensity-modulated radiation therapy (IMRT) or volumetric modulated arc therapy (VMAT). Causes DNA double-strand breaks in residual tumor cells, inducing cell death. Used adjuvantly after surgery for high-risk features or palliatively for symptomatic metastases. IMPORTANT: Radioactive iodine (RAI/I-131) has NO role in MTC — C-cells do not concentrate iodine.

key_evidence.study_name: NCCN Guidelines / ATA MTC Guidelines — Adjuvant EBRT

treatments.availability: Universally available at radiation oncology centers. No RAI role in MTC

treatments.name: Selpercatinib Neoadjuvant Therapy (Pre-surgical RET Inhibition)

evidence_level.rationale: Case reports and small series only. Two published case reports of pralsetinib as neoadjuvant. Selpercatinib neoadjuvant data limited to case series showing tumor reductions of 24-55%. No prospective trials. Growing clinical interest but minimal evidence base

survival_benefit.rationale: Case reports show tumor volume reductions of 24-55% enabling R0/R1 resection in previously unresectable cases. Concept of converting unresectable to resectable MTC is compelling. May improve surgical outcomes and biochemical cure rates. Particularly relevant if this T3N1a has challenging surgical anatomy

accessibility.rationale: Selpercatinib is FDA approved — neoadjuvant use is off-label but feasible at any center with RET testing capability. Requires multidisciplinary decision-making between endocrine surgery and medical oncology

safety_profile.rationale: Selpercatinib safety profile well-characterized. Neoadjuvant use for 3-6 months pre-surgery carries manageable risk. Concern about wound healing if surgery is performed while on drug — washout period needed. Theoretical risk of accelerated progression upon discontinuation

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires RET-mutant MTC — patient has confirmed RET M918T. Perfect biomarker match. Neoadjuvant approach specifically leverages the high response rate in treatment-naive RET M918T disease

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pre-surgical administration of selpercatinib to reduce tumor burden and facilitate more complete surgical resection. Aims to shrink primary tumor and involved lymph nodes, potentially converting R1/R2 resection to R0, and improving rates of biochemical cure (undetectable postoperative calcitonin).

key_evidence.study_name: Case Reports of Neoadjuvant RET Inhibition in MTC

notable_side_effects: Same as selpercatinib (hypertension, diarrhea, dry mouth, ALT/AST elevation)
Potential wound healing concerns — washout period before surgery needed
Theoretical risk of rebound progression upon discontinuation
treatments.availability: Off-label use of FDA-approved selpercatinib. No prospective trial data. Requires multidisciplinary discussion

treatments.name: Lenvatinib (Lenvima) — Multi-Kinase Inhibitor (Off-Label)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trial (n=59) in advanced MTC published 2016. Not FDA-approved for MTC (approved for DTC). Used off-label as salvage therapy after progression on cabozantinib/vandetanib. Real-world data supports utility in MKI-resistant MTC

survival_benefit.rationale: Phase 2: ORR 36%, disease control rate 80%, median PFS 9.0 months. Modest activity as salvage therapy. No Phase 3 data in MTC. Useful primarily when other options exhausted

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for differentiated thyroid cancer but off-label for MTC. Available at oncology centers but insurance coverage for off-label MTC use may be challenging. Requires oncologist justification

safety_profile.rationale: Similar toxicity profile to other MKIs: hypertension, diarrhea, decreased appetite, weight loss, fatigue. Proteinuria and palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia common. Grade >=3 AEs frequent

biomarker_match.rationale: Multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR1-3, FGFR1-4, PDGFR-alpha, RET, and KIT. Not selective for RET but has anti-RET activity. Broader kinase coverage may help with resistance mechanisms

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Oral multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR1-3, FGFR1-4, PDGFR-alpha, RET, and KIT. Provides potent anti-angiogenic activity through VEGFR/FGFR blockade with additional direct anti-tumor effects via RET inhibition. Broader kinase spectrum than cabozantinib.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Study of Lenvatinib in Advanced MTC

biomarker_requirements: None required — multi-kinase inhibitor with broad activity
treatments.availability: FDA Approved for DTC (off-label for MTC). Available but insurance coverage variable for MTC indication

treatments.name: SY-5007 — Next-Generation Selective RET Inhibitor

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1 dose-escalation and dose-expansion study (n=122, including 23 MTC patients). Published in Nature Medicine 2024. Promising early efficacy data but no Phase 2/3 trials yet. Designed to overcome resistance to first-generation RET inhibitors

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 52.2% in MTC patients in Phase 1 — notable given many were pretreated with prior RET inhibitors. Designed to target solvent front mutations (G810) that cause resistance to selpercatinib. Potential to extend survival after first-generation RET inhibitor failure

accessibility.rationale: Phase 1 clinical trial only. Available only at select trial sites, primarily in China. No FDA or EMA approval. Years from potential commercial availability

safety_profile.rationale: Reported to exhibit superior tolerability compared to selpercatinib and pralsetinib in early data. Selective RET inhibition with reduced off-target effects. Toxicity profile still being characterized

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets pan-RET mutations including M918T and resistance mutations (G810 solvent front). Particularly valuable for patients who develop resistance to selpercatinib via solvent front mutations. Excellent match for RET M918T

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Next-generation selective RET inhibitor designed with activity against both wild-type and mutant RET, including solvent front mutations (G810C/S/R) and gatekeeper mutations (V804L/M) that confer resistance to first-generation selective RET inhibitors selpercatinib and pralsetinib. Maintains potency against the M918T activating mutation.
biomarker_requirements: Particularly relevant for RET inhibitor-refractory patients with solvent front mutations
notable_side_effects: Full toxicity profile still under characterization
treatments.availability: Phase 1 clinical trial — primarily in China. Not commercially available

treatments.name: Peptide Receptor Radionuclide Therapy (PRRT) with 177Lu-DOTATATE

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple retrospective studies and small prospective series (total n=186 across 11 studies). No randomized trials in MTC specifically. Approved for GEP-NETs (Lutathera) but off-label for MTC. Evidence base growing but limited by heterogeneous study designs

survival_benefit.rationale: Disease control rate 62.4% (CR 2.6%, PR 5.1%, SD 54.7%). Biochemical response: 28.6% showed >=50% calcitonin decrease. Primarily stabilizes disease rather than inducing remissions. Longer survival in responders but no comparative data

accessibility.rationale: 177Lu-DOTATATE (Lutathera) FDA approved for GEP-NETs but off-label for MTC. Requires SSTR-positive disease confirmed on 68Ga-DOTATATE PET/CT. Available at specialized nuclear medicine centers. Insurance coverage uncertain for MTC

safety_profile.rationale: Generally well tolerated with low acute toxicity. Main risks: myelosuppression (thrombocytopenia, lymphopenia), nephrotoxicity (cumulative), nausea. Renal protective amino acid infusion reduces nephrotoxicity. Favorable compared to MKIs

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires somatostatin receptor expression confirmed on SSTR PET/CT. Not all MTC tumors are SSTR-positive. RET M918T status not directly relevant — depends on SSTR density. Additional imaging required to assess eligibility

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Targeted radionuclide therapy using 177Lutetium-labeled somatostatin analogues (DOTATATE) that bind to somatostatin receptors (SSTR) overexpressed on MTC cells. Delivers localized beta radiation to tumor cells, causing DNA damage and apoptosis while sparing surrounding tissue. 177Lu has a 3mm tissue penetration range ideal for small metastases.

key_evidence.study_name: Systematic Review of PRRT in MTC
key_evidence.journal: Journal of Clinical Medicine / BMC Cancer

biomarker_requirements: Somatostatin receptor expression confirmed on 68Ga-DOTATATE PET/CT
RET status not directly relevant for PRRT eligibility
notable_side_effects: Nephrotoxicity (cumulative, reduced with amino acid infusion)
Rare: myelodysplastic syndrome with cumulative doses
treatments.availability: Off-label use of FDA-approved 177Lu-DOTATATE (Lutathera). Requires SSTR-positive disease on PET/CT. Available at specialized nuclear medicine centers

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) — Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trial NCT03072160 (n=17) completed. Negative results — no radiographic responses observed, no 50% decline in calcitonin or CEA. Study closed early. Single-agent checkpoint inhibition appears ineffective in MTC

survival_benefit.rationale: No objective responses in either cohort (with or without prior vaccine therapy). No calcitonin or CEA decline. MTC generally has low tumor mutational burden and immunologically cold microenvironment. No survival benefit demonstrated

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for multiple indications (not MTC). Widely available. Off-label use for MTC theoretically possible but not supported by evidence. Not recommended per current guidelines

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized safety profile from extensive use in other cancers. Immune-related AEs: thyroiditis (particularly relevant in thyroid cancer patients), pneumonitis, colitis, hepatitis. Generally manageable with corticosteroids

biomarker_match.rationale: MTC generally has low PD-L1 expression and low TMB. RET M918T status does not predict immunotherapy response. May have a role in rare MSI-H/dMMR MTC (tissue-agnostic approval) but this is exceedingly rare

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks the interaction between PD-1 on T cells and PD-L1/PD-L2 on tumor cells, re-activating anti-tumor immune responses. In MTC, the immunologically cold tumor microenvironment and low PD-L1 expression limit the effectiveness of single-agent PD-1 blockade.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Trial of Pembrolizumab in Recurrent/Metastatic MTC (NCT03072160)

biomarker_requirements: No validated predictive biomarker in MTC
PD-L1 expression generally low in MTC
MSI-H/dMMR status (rare in MTC) may predict benefit via tissue-agnostic approval
treatments.availability: FDA Approved for multiple indications (not MTC). Off-label use not recommended based on negative Phase 2 data

treatments.name: TY-1091 — Next-Generation Selective RET Inhibitor

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1 trial initiated in China (recruiting since April 2023, estimated completion June 2025). Preclinical data presented at AACR 2023. No published clinical efficacy data yet. Promising preclinical activity against resistance mutations

survival_benefit.rationale: Preclinical data only. Demonstrates superior antitumor activity in multiple RET-mutant models compared to first-generation RET inhibitors. Clinical benefit in humans not yet established

accessibility.rationale: Early Phase 1 clinical trial in China only. No commercial availability. Estimated 4-6 years from potential approval if development proceeds favorably

safety_profile.rationale: Preclinical safety data favorable. Designed for high selectivity against RET to minimize off-target effects. Clinical safety profile unknown

biomarker_match.rationale: Designed as a potent inhibitor against pan-RET mutations including M918T, solvent front (G810), and gatekeeper (V804) resistance mutations. High relevance for this patient's molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Highly selective and potent second-generation oral RET inhibitor. Designed to overcome resistance to selpercatinib and pralsetinib by maintaining activity against solvent front mutations (G810C/S/R) and gatekeeper mutations (V804L/M). Demonstrates superior antitumor activity in preclinical RET-mutant models.
biomarker_requirements: Designed specifically for RET inhibitor-refractory disease
notable_side_effects: Clinical safety profile not yet established
Preclinical data suggest favorable selectivity profile
treatments.availability: Phase 1 clinical trial in China. Not commercially available anywhere

treatments.name: Nivolumab + Ipilimumab — Dual Checkpoint Inhibition

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 study with MTC exploratory cohort. Very limited data in MTC specifically. Primary endpoint was for radioiodine-refractory DTC; MTC was a secondary cohort. Combination checkpoint blockade theoretically more potent than monotherapy but unproven in MTC

survival_benefit.rationale: Very limited published efficacy data for the MTC cohort. Single-agent pembrolizumab failed in MTC (ORR 0%). Dual checkpoint inhibition may overcome resistance in immunologically cold tumors, but this is speculative in MTC. No demonstrated survival benefit

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs FDA approved for other indications. Off-label combination use possible but not supported by MTC-specific evidence. High cost of dual immunotherapy. Clinical trial access limited

safety_profile.rationale: Dual checkpoint inhibition carries higher toxicity than monotherapy. Grade >=3 immune-related AEs in 40-60% of patients in melanoma trials. Colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies more frequent and severe. Significant toxicity concern in MTC where benefit is unproven

biomarker_match.rationale: MTC generally immunologically cold with low PD-L1 and low TMB. Dual checkpoint blockade may partially overcome immune exclusion but no evidence of biomarker-driven response in RET M918T MTC. Speculative approach

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab (anti-PD-1) combined with ipilimumab (anti-CTLA-4) provides dual checkpoint blockade. Ipilimumab enhances T-cell priming and activation in lymph nodes while nivolumab blocks PD-1-mediated immune suppression in the tumor microenvironment. The combination aims to overcome the immunologically cold microenvironment of MTC.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Nivolumab + Ipilimumab in Thyroid Cancer (MTC exploratory cohort)

biomarker_requirements: High TMB or MSI-H may predict benefit (rare in MTC)
treatments.availability: Both drugs FDA approved for other indications. Clinical trial access for MTC limited. Off-label use not recommended

clinical_trials.title: LIBRETTO-531: Phase 3 Study of Selpercatinib vs Cabozantinib or Vandetanib in Advanced RET-Mutant Medullary Thyroid Cancer
clinical_trials.early_results: Primary endpoint met: PFS HR 0.28 (p<0.001). ORR 69.4% vs 38.8%. FDA traditional approval granted September 2024 based on these results. Study continuing for OS maturity.

clinical_trials.title: Phase 1 Study of SY-5007 in Patients with Advanced RET-Altered Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 52.2% in MTC (n=23). Superior tolerability reported vs first-generation RET inhibitors. Dose-expansion ongoing.

clinical_trials.title: Phase 1/2 Study of TY-1091 in Advanced RET-Altered Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: Preclinical data: superior antitumor activity in multiple RET-mutant models vs first-generation RET inhibitors. Active against G810 solvent front and V804 gatekeeper resistance mutations. Clinical data pending.

clinical_trials.title: Phase 2 Study of Pembrolizumab in Recurrent or Metastatic Medullary Thyroid Cancer
biomarker_requirements: Two cohorts: with and without prior vaccine therapy
clinical_trials.early_results: NEGATIVE: No objective responses (ORR 0%). No 50% decline in calcitonin or CEA. Study closed early. Single-agent pembrolizumab ineffective in MTC.

clinical_trials.title: Expanded Access for LOXO-260 in Participants with RET-Altered Cancer Refractory to RET Inhibitor Treatment
clinical_trials.status: Discontinued (Eli Lilly removed LOXO-260 from pipeline early 2024)
biomarker_requirements: Refractory to prior RET inhibitor therapy
clinical_trials.early_results: Program discontinued. LOXO-260 removed from Lilly pipeline as part of Q4 2023 portfolio review. No clinical efficacy data published.

clinical_trials.title: Study of HA121-28 in Patients with Advanced RET-Altered Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: Next-generation RET inhibitor with activity against resistance mutations. Early-phase data pending publication.

combination_strategies.rationale: MET amplification identified as a resistance mechanism to RET inhibitors. Selpercatinib + crizotinib combination successfully overcame MET-mediated escape in at least one reported case. Addresses bypass signaling that drives acquired resistance to selective RET inhibition.

Sequential approach: neoadjuvant selpercatinib to reduce tumor burden (24-55% volume reduction reported), followed by surgery for potential biochemical cure, with adjuvant selpercatinib for persistent calcitonin elevation. Integrates targeted therapy with the only curative modality for MTC.

Cabozantinib inhibits VEGFR and MET, which can remodel the tumor microenvironment and enhance T-cell infiltration. Theoretical synergy with anti-PD-1/PD-L1 antibodies. Demonstrated in other tumor types (RCC, HCC) but unproven in MTC. MTC's immunologically cold phenotype is a major barrier.

For SSTR-positive MTC progressing on selpercatinib, sequential or combination PRRT could provide non-cross-resistant anti-tumor activity via direct radionuclide-mediated DNA damage. Different mechanism of action could address clonal heterogeneity. Requires SSTR-positive disease on 68Ga-DOTATATE PET/CT.

Sequential use of MKIs after progression on first-line. Different kinase selectivity profiles (cabozantinib targets MET; vandetanib targets EGFR) provide rationale for non-cross-resistance. Real-world data show some patients benefit from second MKI after first MKI failure. Concurrent use not recommended due to overlapping toxicities.

supportive_care.approach: Calcitonin and CEA Doubling Time Monitoring
supportive_care.evidence: ATA 2015 Guidelines, multiple retrospective studies (Barbet et al., JCEM 2005). Calcitonin doubling time <6 months: 5-year survival 25%, 10-year survival 8%. Doubling time >2 years: virtually all patients alive at follow-up. CEA doubling time <24 months associated with aggressive disease
supportive_care.benefit: Critical prognostic tool guiding treatment intensity. Serial calcitonin/CEA measurements every 3-6 months post-surgery enable dynamic risk stratification. Rising calcitonin with short doubling time (<6 months) indicates need for systemic therapy. Stable or slowly rising levels allow active surveillance. CEA doubling time provides independent prognostic information

supportive_care.approach: Germline RET Testing and Genetic Counseling (Rule out MEN2)
supportive_care.evidence: NCCN v1.2025, ATA 2015 Guidelines, GeneReviews. M918T is the mutation associated with MEN2B (most aggressive hereditary MTC syndrome). Even in apparently sporadic MTC, germline RET testing is mandatory to rule out hereditary disease. 1-7% of apparently sporadic MTC has occult germline mutations
supportive_care.benefit: Identifies hereditary MEN2 syndrome requiring: (1) screening for pheochromocytoma (catecholamines/metanephrines — MUST be ruled out BEFORE surgery), (2) hyperparathyroidism screening (MEN2A), (3) cascade genetic testing of first-degree relatives, (4) lifelong surveillance protocol. If M918T is germline = MEN2B diagnosis with associated phenotypic features
supportive_care.recommendation_level: Strong (NCCN Category 1, ATA Grade A — mandatory for all MTC patients)

supportive_care.approach: Thyroid Hormone Replacement and Calcium/Vitamin D Supplementation
supportive_care.evidence: Standard endocrine practice guidelines. Post-total thyroidectomy requires lifelong levothyroxine. Post-parathyroidectomy or hypoparathyroidism requires calcium and calcitriol. NCCN and Endocrine Society guidelines
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains euthyroid state (TSH target: normal range — no TSH suppression needed for MTC unlike DTC). Prevents symptomatic hypocalcemia post-surgery. Calcitriol preferred over ergocalciferol for hypoparathyroidism. Bone density preservation

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Distress Management Guidelines. 45-year-old male with aggressive RET M918T mutation and regional lymph node metastasis requires psychological support for disease management, treatment decision-making, and potential hereditary cancer implications
supportive_care.benefit: Improved treatment adherence, quality of life, and coping. Addresses anxiety related to lifelong monitoring, potential genetic implications for family members, and treatment decision-making between immediate surgery vs neoadjuvant approach

supportive_care.evidence: Clinical practice guidelines for MTC and TKI-associated diarrhea. Calcitonin hypersecretion in MTC causes secretory diarrhea independent of treatment. TKIs (cabozantinib 63%, vandetanib 56%) and selpercatinib (37%) further exacerbate diarrhea
supportive_care.benefit: Improved quality of life and treatment adherence. Loperamide first-line; octreotide (somatostatin analogue) for refractory secretory diarrhea from calcitonin excess. Dietary modifications (low-fat, lactose-free). Electrolyte monitoring. Distinguishing disease-related from treatment-related diarrhea guides management
supportive_care.recommendation_level: Strong (standard supportive care for MTC patients)

supportive_care.evidence: Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guidelines. Post-thyroidectomy hypoparathyroidism and long-term TKI use (especially cabozantinib targeting VEGFR) affect bone metabolism. MTC bone metastases require monitoring
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents osteoporosis and pathologic fractures. DEXA scan baseline and every 1-2 years. Calcium, vitamin D, and calcitriol supplementation as needed. Denosumab or zoledronic acid for bone metastases if they develop. Weight-bearing exercise recommended
supportive_care.recommendation_level: Moderate (guideline-supported for post-thyroidectomy and TKI-treated patients)
